# Region categorization oracles

Hand-curated test oracles for the two-facet region categorization derivation
(`src/pgm_map_studio/studio/services/region_categorizer.py`, contract
`docs/contracts/region-categorization.md`). Each `<map>.json` maps a **named**
region id to its expected `{category, roles}`; `inputs/<map>.json` is the vendored
`xml_data.json` the derivation runs on (so the test is hermetic — no live corpus).

Per contract §9 a fixture is a *proposed* labeling until a CTW author signs off,
at which point it becomes a hard oracle.

| Map | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `annealing_iv` | **verified** (rockymine, 2026-06-10) | 4-team; all core patterns. The authoritative correctness oracle. |
| `acapulco` | **verified** (rockymine, 2026-06-10; `rule_group` on the woolroom unions confirmed) | multi-wool 2-team. |
| `icecream_sandwiched_ii` | **verified** (rockymine, 2026-06-10; `build` for water-lanes confirmed) | time-gated build + non-wool (golden-apple) spawner mechanic + message-derived spawn zones. |
| `vertex` | **verified** (rockymine, 2026-06-10; build-mechanics + mirror + spawner confirmed) | clean 2-team; diagonal mirror; permissive-placement build (`playable-area`). |

Proposed fixtures are generated by the derivation and committed as regression
locks: the test asserts the derivation still reproduces them, so any future change
that shifts these maps is surfaced for author re-review. Verifying/correcting a
proposed fixture turns it into an oracle — edit the JSON to the correct labels and
move its row to **verified**.

Resolved from rockymine's 2026-06-10 review:
- `icecream` `gap-spawner*` → `mechanic` (the spawner dispenses golden apples, not wool).
- `icecream` `blue-prot`/`red-prot`/`spawn-protection` → `spawn` (apply messages name the
  enemy spawn; `spawn-protection` also matches the break-floor-material + deny-place pattern).
- `vertex` diagonal-mirror regions were rendering rotated (point-flip instead of reflection across
  the 45° axis); fixed in `pgm.regions.reflect_*` + `region_encoder`, verified flush in-browser.

- `vertex` `playable-area`/`blue-side`/`red-side` → `build`: the global rule uses `playable-area`
  as a `block_place` *filter* (permissive placement = the buildable floor); confirmed by rockymine's
  build-mechanics explanation (stone floor + `deny-bottom-layer` quirk).
